title: Telecommunications in Kenya
content:
Telecommunications in Kenya include radio, television, fixed and mobile telephones, and the Internet. Radio and television. Radio stations: Radio s: 3.07 million (1997). Television stations: Television set s: 730,000 (1997). Television is the main news source in cities and towns. TV in rural areas is limited by lack of reliable electricity and radio listening dominates in rural areas, where most Kenyans live.

A switchover to digital TV is under way. Satellite pay-TV is offered by the Wananchi Group, which operates Zuku TV, and by South Africa's MultiChoice. Entertainment, music and phone-ins dominate the radio scene, which includes Islamic stations and stations broadcasting in local languages. The BBC World Service is available in Nairobi, Mombasa, and Kisumu. The state-run Kenya Broadcasting Corporation (KBC) is funded from advertising revenue and from the government. Telephones.
